scheme_name: Grant of Stipends to the Physically Handicapped Students
page_title: Grant of Stipends to the Physically Handicapped Students

details: The scheme "Grant of Stipends to the Physically Handicapped Students" by the Directorate of Social Welfare, Government of Goa aims to improve the educational status of handicapped children studying in standards 1st to 10th by providing financial assistance. Through this scheme, monthly stipends ranging from ₹200 to ₹300 are provided to parents who are unable to send their children to school due to economic reasons. The applications for this scheme are accepted online. The scheme is being implemented by Chief Minister Scholarship Portal, Government of Goa.
eligibility: The applicant must be a bonafide resident of the State of Goa
The applicant must be a regular student of a recognized school
The applicant's parents/guardian's combined annual income should not exceed ₹25,000
The applicant must be physically handicapped as specified under Rule 4 read with Rule 5
The applicant should not be a repeater in the current standard (one failure can be condoned at Director's discretion)
The applicant should not be residing in or receiving maintenance from any government institutions, local bodies, or grant-receiving private organizations
The applicant should not be receiving any other stipend or scholarship
Multiple handicapped children from the same family are eligible if other conditions are met

online: For Registration
Step 1:
Visit the
Official Website
of the Chief Minister Scholarship Portal, Government of Goa.
Step 2:
At the top right corner of the landing page, click "Register". You will be taken to the
registration page
Step 3:
On the registration page, under the "Student Registration" section, click "Click Here".
In the Student Registration Form, fill in the following fields:

Student's Name, Gender, Category, Date of Birth (DD-MM-YYYY), Aadhaar Number, House Number, Waddo/Street, Village Panchayat/Municipality, District, Taluka, Assembly/Constituency, City/Town/Village, PIN Code, Mobile Number, Email ID.
Step 4:
Create a password ranging between 8 to 15 characters and confirm the same.
Step 5:
Click "Register" and then click "Yes" to the Confirmation Popup.
For Application
Upon successful registration, visit the
At the top right corner of the landing page, click "
Login
On the login page, fill in your username, password, captcha code, and click "Login".
Upon logging in, a list of eligible schemes will be displayed. Click the scheme that you intend to apply for.
In the "Apply" section on the side navigation bar, click "Fresh Application".
Step 6:
Fill in all the mandatory fields of the Application Form and upload all the required documents in the specified format and size and click "Submit".
Post Application Process
1. After successful submission of the application, your school/institution will verify the application and submit it to the directorate for further scrutiny.
2. In case of an issue in the application form or documents uploaded, the school/institution can send back the application to the student for necessary corrections and resubmission.
Track Your Application
In the side navigation bar, click "
Track Application
On the next page, provide your Aadhaar Number and Date of Birth, and click "Submit".

documents_required: Caste Certificate (Valid for 3 years from date of issue)
Income Certificate (Valid for 3 years from date of issue)

faqs.question: What is the objective of the Grant of Stipends to the Physically Handicapped Students scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme aims to improve the educational status of physically handicapped children studying in classes I to X by providing financial assistance to parents who are otherwise unable to send their children to school due to economic reasons.

faqs.question: Who is eligible for the stipend under this scheme?
faqs.answer: To be eligible: The student must be a bonafide resident of Goa. The student must be a regular student at a recognized school. The combined income of the parents/guardian should not exceed ₹25,000 per annum. The student must not be residing in or receiving maintenance from any government, local body, or grant-aided private institution. The student should not be receiving any other stipend or scholarship from another source. All handicapped children from the same family are eligible, provided they meet the conditions.

faqs.question: What are the conditions for physically handicapped students to qualify for the stipend?
faqs.answer: The applicant must fulfil the physical handicap criteria specified under Rule 4 and Rule 5 of the scheme's rules.

faqs.question: Are students who repeat a grade eligible for the stipend?
faqs.answer: Students repeating a grade are generally not eligible for the stipend. However, one failure can be condoned at the discretion of the Director.

faqs.question: What is the financial assistance provided under the scheme?
faqs.answer: The stipend rates are: ₹200 per month for students in classes I to VIII. ₹300 per month for students in classes IX to XII.

faqs.question: How long is the stipend paid to the eligible students?
faqs.answer: The stipend is paid for the full academic year, which is 11 months and is disbursed in two instalments—one in September and the second in January.

faqs.question: Can a student receive the stipend if they are already getting another scholarship?
faqs.answer: No, students who are receiving a stipend or scholarship from any other source are not eligible for this scheme.

faqs.question: What documents are required to apply for this stipend?
faqs.answer: The required documents include: Aadhaar Card Caste Certificate (valid for 3 years from the date of issue) Income Certificate (valid for 3 years from the date of issue) Marksheet or Certificate Proof of Fees Paid Bank Account details

faqs.question: Are multiple handicapped children from the same family eligible for this stipend?
faqs.answer: Yes, if a family has more than one physically handicapped child, all such children are eligible for the stipend, provided they meet the scheme’s other conditions.

faqs.question: When are the stipends disbursed during the academic year?
faqs.answer: The stipend is disbursed in two instalments: the first in September and the second in January.
